summary: The S&P 500 closed Monday at 7,554.29, up 1.65%, as the Nasdaq surged 3.1%. This sharp rally appeared closely tied to geopolitical developments. headline: The Mag 7 plus these three AI companies make up the new Fab 10
summary: The Magnificent Seven plus SpaceX (SPCX), Anthropic (ANTH.PVT), and OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) equal the Fab 10. Could this trio of AI mega-IPOs bring enthusiasm back to the tech space previously dominated by the Mag 7? Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Ines Ferré and Innovator From Goldman Sachs Asset Management chief investment strategist Tim Urbanowicz weigh in on the renewed investor sentiment these companies could bring to the AI trade. The Magnificent Seven consists of Meta Platforms (META), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL, GOOG), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), and Elon Musk's Tesla (TSLA).

content.title: The Mag 7 plus these three AI companies make up the new Fab 10

The Magnificent Seven plus SpaceX (SPCX), Anthropic (ANTH.PVT), and OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) equal the Fab 10. Could this trio of AI mega-IPOs bring enthusiasm back to the tech space previously dominated by the Mag 7?

Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Ines Ferré and Innovator From Goldman Sachs Asset Management chief investment strategist Tim Urbanowicz weigh in on the renewed investor sentiment these companies could bring to the AI trade.

The Magnificent Seven consists of Meta Platforms (META), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL, GOOG), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), and Elon Musk's Tesla (TSLA).

headline: Former Microsoft chief Barclay joins British fintech unicorn Zilch
summary: One of Britain's leading fintech unicorns will this week name Microsoft's former UK boss to its board as it continues to seek acquisition targets ahead of an eventual public listing.  Sky News understands that Zilch, which has been valued at about $2bn, will this week announce that Dame Clare Barclay has been appointed as a non-executive director.  Dame Clare, who recently left Microsoft after 27 years which included stints as UK country manager and president of enterprise and industry in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, also serves as chair of the government's Industrial Strategy Advisory Council.

content.title: Former Microsoft chief Barclay joins British fintech unicorn Zilch
content.summary: One of Britain's leading fintech unicorns will this week name Microsoft's former UK boss to its board as it continues to seek acquisition targets ahead of an eventual public listing.  Sky News understands that Zilch, which has been valued at about $2bn, will this week announce that Dame Clare Barclay has been appointed as a non-executive director.  Dame Clare, who recently left Microsoft after 27 years which included stints as UK country manager and president of enterprise and industry in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, also serves as chair of the government's Industrial Strategy Advisory Council.

headline: Tech Weekly: A blockbuster IPO, a sweeping online block
summary:
STORY: From a record-breaking IPO to a far-reaching online ban, this is Tech Weekly. 

:: Tech Weekly

SpaceX on Thursday priced the biggest-ever U.S. initial public offering, raising a record $75 billion. 

It’s made Elon Musk’s rocket and spacecraft manufacturer one of the world’s most valuable companies.

Selling 555.56 million shares at $135 apiece, the company was valued at $1.77 trillion. 

Thursday’s pricing supports Musk's most ambitious project yet - even as some analysts question whether its lofty valuation is justified.

The company said Monday its underwriters had exercised the so-called "greenshoe" option to purchase additional shares.

Microsoft has been sued by shareholders who say they've been defrauded. 

They’ve said the tech giant inflated its stock price by failing to disclose slowing growth in its Azure cloud business.

And failing to disclose the need to spend billions of dollars on AI infrastructure. 

The proposed class action, filed Friday, was prompted by Microsoft shares falling 10% on January 29 after an earnings report.

About $357 billion of market value was erased, and Microsoft's stock suffered its biggest one-day decline in nearly six years.

Microsoft said on Monday it believes the claims are "without merit”. 

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday he would ban social media sites for under-16s..

And impose restrictions on gaming and livestreaming platforms for the age group. 

This amounts to some of the world's most far-reaching online restrictions, with the ban coming into place by next spring.

He said this would help protect children online while pushing back against the power of big technology companies.

"At the moment, strangers can contact any child unchecked. Just think about that. Is there a situation in the offline world where you would just let your child pair up with a stranger, an adult that you don't know anything about?”

Ford and other automakers are scrambling to obtain U.S. government authorization to continue selling certain models - despite being on the market for years. 

It’s due to a U.S. ban on Chinese-developed and -maintained software in connected vehicles. 

Ford told Reuters it’s asked the U.S. Commerce Department for authorization to continue importing its China-built Lincoln Nautilus SUV.

And said despite its tech being built in the U.S., its installation in China now requires government approval. 

Lawmakers have ​proposed making the rules even tougher.

content.title: Tech Weekly: A blockbuster IPO, a sweeping online block
content.summary:
STORY: From a record-breaking IPO to a far-reaching online ban, this is Tech Weekly. 

:: Tech Weekly

SpaceX on Thursday priced the biggest-ever U.S. initial public offering, raising a record $75 billion. 

It’s made Elon Musk’s rocket and spacecraft manufacturer one of the world’s most valuable companies.

Selling 555.56 million shares at $135 apiece, the company was valued at $1.77 trillion. 

Thursday’s pricing supports Musk's most ambitious project yet - even as some analysts question whether its lofty valuation is justified.

The company said Monday its underwriters had exercised the so-called "greenshoe" option to purchase additional shares.

Microsoft has been sued by shareholders who say they've been defrauded. 

They’ve said the tech giant inflated its stock price by failing to disclose slowing growth in its Azure cloud business.

And failing to disclose the need to spend billions of dollars on AI infrastructure. 

The proposed class action, filed Friday, was prompted by Microsoft shares falling 10% on January 29 after an earnings report.

About $357 billion of market value was erased, and Microsoft's stock suffered its biggest one-day decline in nearly six years.

Microsoft said on Monday it believes the claims are "without merit”. 

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday he would ban social media sites for under-16s..

And impose restrictions on gaming and livestreaming platforms for the age group. 

This amounts to some of the world's most far-reaching online restrictions, with the ban coming into place by next spring.

He said this would help protect children online while pushing back against the power of big technology companies.

"At the moment, strangers can contact any child unchecked. Just think about that. Is there a situation in the offline world where you would just let your child pair up with a stranger, an adult that you don't know anything about?”

Ford and other automakers are scrambling to obtain U.S. government authorization to continue selling certain models - despite being on the market for years. 

It’s due to a U.S. ban on Chinese-developed and -maintained software in connected vehicles. 

Ford told Reuters it’s asked the U.S. Commerce Department for authorization to continue importing its China-built Lincoln Nautilus SUV.

And said despite its tech being built in the U.S., its installation in China now requires government approval. 

Lawmakers have ​proposed making the rules even tougher.

headline: Catalent Launches Qai™ to Reimagine Quality Assurance for its Manufacturing Services
summary: TAMPA, Fla., June 16, 2026--Catalent announces the launch of Qai, an enterprise AI tool designed to elevate the best quality systems and processes across Catalent’s network.

content.title: Catalent Launches Qai™ to Reimagine Quality Assurance for its Manufacturing Services
content.summary: TAMPA, Fla., June 16, 2026--Catalent announces the launch of Qai, an enterprise AI tool designed to elevate the best quality systems and processes across Catalent’s network.

thumbnail.caption: CatalentQai™, an AI-enabled solution, strengthens quality management system processes—such as deviations and complaints—by harnessing Catalent’s enterprise data to accelerate analysis, root cause identification and corrective and preventive action development, while improving consistency and speed. Qai represents the first enterprise AI solution launched at Catalent, marking a significant step in its broader effort to implement advanced technologies to drive Patient First outcomes.

headline: Residence Acquires Influencer Agency  GateMaker
summary: GateMaker was founded in 2021 by Ashton Wall and Amelia Soohoo and has worked with brands such as Amika, Estee Lauder Companies, Glossier, LG, L'Occitane en Provence, Milk Makeup, NARS, NEST, Pacifica, Rag & Bone, Reebok, Starbucks, and more.

headline: Investors look beyond the 'Magnificent 7' as Wall Street embraces the 'FAB 10'
summary: SpaceX's record-breaking market debut has pushed Wall Street to widen its favourite shorthand of the so-called 'Magnificent 7', with strategists folding Elon Musk's firm, OpenAI and Anthropic into a new 'FAB 10' label that captures the next phase of the AI trade.View on euronews

headline: Valona Intelligence Launches MCP Server to Bring Trusted Market Intelligence into the Enterprise AI Ecosystem
summary: Valona Intelligence today announced the launch of its Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. MCP is the open standard that allows AI systems to connect to external data sources, making Valona's market and competitive intelligence available directly inside Microsoft Copilot, Claude and other enterprise AI tools and agent frameworks.

content.title: Valona Intelligence Launches MCP Server to Bring Trusted Market Intelligence into the Enterprise AI Ecosystem

headline: EeroQ Makes World-First Breakthrough in Electron Qubits Floating on Helium
summary: EeroQ, the quantum computing company, today announced the first-ever demonstration of the strong coupling of a microwave photon to the charge qubit state of an electron on helium, a landmark result that establishes the electron-on-helium (eHe) approach to quantum computing as a reality and introduces a powerful new contender in the global race to build a practical quantum computer. The results have been peer reviewed and published in Nature Physics.

The results have been peer reviewed and published in Nature Physics.

headline: Howard Marks: Investing in AI Stocks Is ‘Closer to Speculating’ Than Analysis
summary: Although the AI buildout has minted trillions in fresh market value across Wall Street, Howard Marks thinks buyers of these stocks are kidding themselves about what they actually own. On a recent Prof G Markets appearance, Oaktree Capital co-founder Marks laid out a spectrum running from “analytical investing in prosaic, understandable companies” to “speculative investing ... Howard Marks: Investing in AI Stocks Is ‘Closer to Speculating’ Than Analysis

headline: SpaceX Approaches $3 Trillion Market Value as Post-IPO Rally Continues
summary: SpaceX (NASDAQ:SPCX) shares continued their remarkable advance in premarket trading on Tuesday, building on strong gains from their first two sessions as a public company and pushing the aerospace group toward a market capitalization of nearly $3 trillion. The rocket and satellite operator completed the largest initial public offering in history last week, ending its first day of trading on Friday with a valuation of approximately $2.

content.title: SpaceX Approaches $3 Trillion Market Value as Post-IPO Rally Continues

headline: SpaceX vaults past Microsoft and Amazon's market value as post IPO momentum builds
summary: Shares of Elon Musk's SpaceX climbed more than 14% on Tuesday, lifting its valuation past Amazon.com and briefly above Microsoft to rank it among the top-five most ‌valuable companies within days of a blockbuster debut.  Shares of the rockets-to-AI company were last up 14.3% at $220, jumping ‌more than 62% above the $135 IPO price and giving the company a market capitalization of around $2.85 trillion if gains hold.  The stock gave the biggest ​boost to the Nasdaq Composite index.
